rationale: The citing paper uses Zhang and Schmitt (2001) in multiple core places: to motivate the sound-meaning structure of Chinese brand naming, to summarize prior findings on phonetic/semantic/phonosemantic effects, and to position its own contribution against prior lab-based work. It also relies on Zhang and Schmitt (2001) operationally by using that paper's definitions to code brand-name types in the dataset. This is a meaningful conceptual and measurement role, not a passing mention.
relation_description: Wu et al. build on Zhang and Schmitt (2001) as a base framework for Chinese brand-name translation and name-type classification, then test related effects in a new empirical setting using market-level automobile sales. The citing study extends the earlier experimental, perception-focused work to behavioral demand outcomes and heterogeneity across COO and market segments.
